steps.title: Define what an overview requires
steps.content: An overview of a commodity chain must show how it connects production to consumption — both endpoints and the link between them. The notes identify the two endpoints: mushroom pickers in Oregon and wealthy consumers in Japan. A choice that shows only one end, defines commodity chains generically, or focuses on historical origins does not qualify as an overview.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — The contemporary matsutake commodity chain has its origins in the 1980s when, according to Tsing, "the scarcity of matsutake in Japan first became clear.": Focuses only on the historical origin of the chain; does not describe what the chain looks like or how it connects its endpoints. ✗
  • Choice B — Commodity chains include the linked production and purchase of commodities, such as the matsutake mushroom, on the world market.: Gives a generic definition of commodity chains in general; does not describe the matsutake chain specifically. ✗
  • Choice C — Decades after the Japanese import of matsutake began, a commodity chain now links matsutake pickers in Oregon with wealthy consumers of the costly mushrooms in Japan.: Connects both endpoints — Oregon pickers and Japanese consumers — showing the chain that joins them. This is a complete overview. ✓
  • Choice D — Wealthy consumers who buy the costly mushrooms in Japan are at one end of the matsutake commodity chain.: Describes only the consumer end; omits the Oregon pickers and gives no sense of the full chain. ✗
That matches Choice C — Decades after the Japanese import of matsutake began, a commodity chain now links matsutake pickers in Oregon with wealthy consumers of the costly mushrooms in Japan..
